title: advertisement
description: These are all words for a notice, picture or film telling people about a product, job or service.

definitions.definition: a notice, picture or film telling people about a product, job or service; an example of something that shows its good qualities; the act of advertising something and making it public:

examples.en: They ran advertisements on TV and on social media.
examples.cn: 他们在电视和社交媒体上投放广告。

examples.en: Dirty streets are no advertisement for a prosperous society.
examples.cn: 肮脏的街道绝不是繁荣社会的景象。

definitions.definition: [U] the business of attracting the attention of the public to somebody/something such as a company, book, film, film star or product; the things that are done to attract attention:

examples.en: There has been a lot of advance publicity for her new film.
examples.cn: 她的新电影尚未上映即大加宣传。

definitions.definition: (informal) a notice, picture or film telling people about a product, job or service:

examples.en: We put an ad on that website.
examples.cn: 我们在那个网站上登了一则广告。

examples.en: an ad for a new chocolate bar
examples.cn: 新品种巧克力棒的广告

definitions.definition: an advertisement on television or on the radio.
definitions.definition_cn: 指电视或电台播放的广告

definitions.definition: a set of advertisements for a particular product or service; activities done in order to increase the sales of a product or service:

examples.en: a special promotion of local products
examples.cn: 当地产品的特别促销活动

examples.en: She works in sales and promotion.
examples.cn: 她从事销售和推广工作。

definitions.definition: a series of short scenes from a film or television programme, shown in advance to advertise it.
